enrichment.summary: Kyle logs a training day in which he swam approximately 9,345 meters in two sessions at an indoor pool, dealing with faulty earphones and a torn swim cap. He covers his meals, equipment issues, flip-turn practice, and plans to ride the next day, all while building toward open-water triathlon readiness when the reservoir opens May 24th.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Loves triathlon because soreness in one muscle group lets you train a different sport
Can't do them in a real swim yet — lacks the fitness to hold breath through them
Angle gives more power but pushes his head down, making breathing awkward — needs more practice
Doesn't yet have the fitness to do more than one sport per day, so staggers them
Plans to bring two next time so he doesn't run out mid-swim

transcript.text: Hello, so here's training with Kyle V-Log for May 6th.
Today I swam 9,345 meters, I think.
I did it in two goes.
The first one I swam, I think it was, I looked at my watch, it said 55 laps in a 15 meter pool, 15.24 meter pool.
Um, so 15 times 55 is 825, and then my other swim was 885.20, so yeah, like 93.45.
Um, I swam, I got out the first time because my earphones that I got, the second pair, they go in and out.
They didn't work on the left side, so I'm going to see if I can return them.
I spent 50 bucks on them last week.
Um, and, uh, um, so I came home to get my original earphones, and those stayed in just fine.
Tomorrow I'm going to need to buy a new swimming cap, though, because I tried to yank it off, but I didn't, because I was tired, I didn't realize I had my goggles on still.
So the goggles weren't letting it come off, and I yanked the top of it right off.
So I have to buy another one.
I think I only had one, so I have to go down to the store and buy another tomorrow.
Um, so, um, yeah, I got up, I think I had a little bit of insomnia from lack of being able to breathe, and I ended up getting it up at, like, 10.30 or something.
And then I went over, had the breakfast burrito at Dodd's Diner, because I just, I don't know, I had the munchies this morning, wanted to eat something tasty.
So I got that, and then got hamster food, which I didn't need.
And then swam, um, I swam until, I just, you know, I didn't do, at first I couldn't do the flip turns.
So I just practiced on the shallow end over and over again, maybe like six or seven times, or six or a dozen times, until I figured it out.
It wasn't that complicated.
You just have to go directly under, pretty much.
I was trying to, like, do some kind of fancy twist thing.
So you just go directly under, and then you can kick off.
But then I tried to actually do it in my swim, but there was no way, because I had to hold my breath.
So then the rest of the lap, even, I was, I was out of breath.
And by the time I finally catch my breath, I'd have to do another flip turn.
So I don't have the fitness to do flip turns.
But so, yeah, I just, uh, swam that whole time, you know, for a few hours.
I don't know how long, and, uh, and then I went to my mom's house and did a light treatment.
And then I have, like, I have bunches of goods, so I got some, I got some Sprite.
I don't know if I'll eat anything else.
I only had a burrito today.
That's all I ate.
I had some, um, I had one water bottle of, uh, of, um, uh, Cytomax drink, and I drank through the whole thing.
And so next time I go swimming, I'm going to make two Cytomax drinks so that I don't run out.
And then I'll just drink the rest of it as a recovery drink when I come home.
Um, because you can't have them sit around for too long for more than a day or two, because they go rotten.
And, uh, so, um, yeah, uh, my legs were totally sore.
Especially my hamstrings were way sore today, because I did all the stretching yesterday.
But, uh, it's great, it's perfect to go swimming on days where your legs are sore.
I love triathlon because you're, you're, it's a great way to even everything out.
You know, when one thing is sore, you can do something else.
So, um, yeah, I'm going to sleep tonight.
It's like 10 o'clock or, yeah.
And I'm going to sleep tonight and, uh, get up and I'm going to do a ride tomorrow because there's,
I'm going to try the Strava feature on my road bike computer tomorrow.
That'll be fun.
Um, and, uh, and, uh, yeah, I love, love exercising all the time.
I got to the point where I was practicing my stroke, my stroke, my arms were just going down.
And then I remembered you're supposed to have an angle like that.
So I tried the angle and I got more power and I, and I could see how I was going faster.
The problem with having an angle though, is it feels like it pushes my head down.
So it's harder to go back and breathe and then it makes it awkward.
So I need to practice having an angle like this without sucking myself down under the water so I can still breathe.
I'm an ambidextrous swimmer.
So I do, um, one going one direction, turning the side and then going the other direction, turning the side to, to help with my overall stroke.
Um, and, uh, yeah, I mean, it's going to keep it like that.
And, uh, that then I can be an ambidextrous as, as I get better and better.
So I feel kind of good and hopefully we'll see how I feel tomorrow.
Cause if I feel okay, I could do a wicked big ride and maybe come home and do a run.
But until, I don't think I have the fitness to do more than one sport a day.
So I'll just stagger it.
One day swim, one day ride, one day run.
And then, yeah, I can't wait until I start to actually be able to do the sports legitly.
You know, I want to be able to swim for an hour and a half when the, when the reservoir opens on May 24th, which is only a couple of weeks away.
So I'm going to train my ass off tomorrow morning.
I have to bring my car in to get fixed.
Okay.
So that's it later.
So that's it later.
So that's it, that's what I'm saying.
So that's it later.
So that's it, that's what I have to do.
